name: MIRROR
description: A hierarchical benchmark for metacognitive calibration in LLMs. 8 experiments across 4 metacognitive levels, 5 behavioral channels, 16 models from 8 labs.
rai:dataCollection: All evaluation instances are generated via API calls to publicly available LLM endpoints. No human subjects were involved in data collection. Questions were generated by LLMs and verified via cross-model agreement (3-model consensus). A stratified human audit of 420 items found a 0.71% genuine auto-labeling error rate.
rai:dataCollectionMissing: No personally identifiable information is collected. No demographic data is present.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The dataset contains only model-generated questions and model responses.
rai:dataSocialImpact: The benchmark evaluates metacognitive calibration in LLMs for agentic safety applications. Findings inform deployment decisions for autonomous AI systems. The knowing-doing gap finding suggests external scaffolding is needed for safe agentic deployment.
rai:dataLimitations: All experiments use temperature=0. The 62% CFR reduction attenuates to 35% at temperature 0.7. 16 models from 8 labs; proprietary models (GPT-4o, Claude) not tested. Human audit shows 0.71% genuine error rate but low inter-annotator agreement on open-ended agentic tasks (Experiments 4, 9). Parse failure rate: 5.9% overall (excluding gemini-2.5-pro outlier).
rai:dataBiases: Question bank generated by LLMs, which may reflect biases in training data. Domain coverage is limited to 8 cognitive domains; real-world agentic tasks span a broader space. The wagering channel uses a symmetric linear scoring rule, which is not strictly proper.
